result.stdout:
Description:
  Universal healthcare standards platform supporting HL7, FHIR, and NCPDP

Usage:
  Pidgeon.CLI [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information
  --verbose       Enable verbose output
  -q, --quiet     Suppress status output (only emit data to stdout)
  --output        Output format: text|json (default: text) [default: text]
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  ai                                      [Pro] AI-powered healthcare message operations (hardware dependent)
  catalog                                 Browse, search, and generate from the scenario catalog
  completions <bash|fish|powershell|zsh>  Generate shell completion setup scripts
  config                                  Manage configuration intelligence
  data                                    Manage clinical terminology data packages
  deident <input> <output>                De-identify real messages/resources (on-device), preserving referential integrity
  diff <paths>                            [Pro] Compare two artifacts (files or folders). Field-aware for HL7; JSON-tree for FHIR. Emits hints.
  fhir-search                             [Pro] Test FHIR search functionality with realistic server simulation
  find <query>                            🔍 Search engine for healthcare message discovery and field location
  flock-audit                             Flock compliance audit trails and reporting.
  flock-ci                                Flock CI/CD integration: deterministic generation and verification.
  flock                                   Generate synthetic patient populations with epidemiological grounding.
  generate <message-types>                Generate synthetic messages/resources with smart standard inference
  golden                                  Clinical acceptance testing suite
  loft                                    Observe and monitor live HL7 interface traffic with real-time validation and alerting.
  lookup <path>                           Look up healthcare standards definitions with smart inference
  path                                    Discover and explore semantic field paths across healthcare standards
  scenario <scenario-type>                [Pro] Generate realistic clinical scenario bundles
  search <resource-type>                  Simulate FHIR search queries with realistic server responses
  send <target>                           Generate and deliver messages to a target path or endpoint
  session                                 Manage sessions for maintaining field values across commands
  set <field> <value>                     Set field values with smart session management
  upgrade                                 Show subscription status and upgrade options
  validate <files>                        Validate healthcare messages against standards
  vendor                                  Browse and inspect curated vendor interface profiles
  workflow                                [Pro] Interactive workflow wizard for multi-step healthcare scenarios
  healing                                 Manage self-healing rules, review queue, and circuit breakers.
  intelligence                            Manage vendor intelligence network: telemetry, profiles, impact reports, consent.

  Pidgeon.CLI config analyze <samples> [options]

Arguments:
  <samples>  Path to file or directory containing sample messages (auto-detects HL7/FHIR/NCPDP)

Options:
  -s, --save            Save inferred configuration to file (JSON format)
  --seed                Deterministic seed for reproducible analysis
  -c, --min-confidence  Minimum confidence threshold (0.0-1.0, default: 0.6)

  --samples             Path to samples (redundant - use positional arg)
  -?, -h, --help        Show help and usage information

Description:
  De-identify real messages/resources (on-device), preserving referential integrity

Usage:
  Pidgeon.CLI deident <input> <output> [options]

Arguments:
  <input>   File or folder of source data
  <output>  Output file/folder (created if missing)

Options:
  -d, --date-shift  Shift dates by +/-N days (e.g., 30d, -14d)
  -k, --keep-ids    Comma-list of identifiers to keep unhashed (e.g., visitId)
  -s, --salt        Salt for deterministic hashing
  -p, --preview     Show sample before/after rows without writing files [default: False]
  -i, --in          File or folder of source data (redundant - use positional args)
  -o, --out         Output file/folder (redundant - use positional args)
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Run Flock generation in CI mode with deterministic output.

Usage:
  Pidgeon.CLI flock-ci generate [options]

Options:
  --ci              Enable CI mode: deterministic seed, quiet output, JSON results [default: True]
  --config          Path to Flock configuration file
  --schema          Path to database schema DDL or JSON
  --connection      Database connection string
  -o, --output      Output directory [default: ./flock-output]
  --seed-from-sha   Git commit SHA to derive deterministic seed
  --seed            Explicit seed value (overrides --seed-from-sha)
  -n, --population  Number of patients to generate [default: 100]
  --format          Output format: sql or csv [default: sql]
  --subset          Enable subsetting mode (requires --connection) [default: False]
  --no-snapshot     Skip automatic version snapshot after generation [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generate synthetic messages/resources with smart standard inference

Usage:
  Pidgeon.CLI generate <message-types>... [options]

Arguments:
  <message-types>  Message type (e.g., ADT^A01, Patient, NewRx) - supports 239+ HL7/FHIR/NCPDP types

Options:
  -c, --count                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         Number of messages to generate [default: 1]
  -o, --output                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Output file path (optional, defaults to console)
  -f, --format <auto|hl7|json|ndjson|xml>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             Output format: auto|hl7|json|ndjson [default: auto]
  -m, --mode <api|model|procedural>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   Generation mode: procedural|model|api [default: procedural]
  -s, --seed                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Deterministic seed for reproducible data
  -v, --vendor <allscripts|allscripts_professional|allscripts_sunrise|athenahealth|cerner|cerner_lab|cerner_millennium|cerner_powerchart|clinic_standard|eclinicalworks|epic|epic_amb|epic_er|epic_ip|epic_lab|hospital_standard|labcorp|lab_standard|meditech|meditech_expanse|meditech_magic|nextgen|quest|vitera>  Apply vendor pattern (e.g., epic, cerner, meditech)
  --hl7-version                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       HL7 version for message generation (2.3|2.3.1|2.4|2.5|2.5.1|2.6|2.7|2.8) or 'list' to show available [default: 2.3]
  --session                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           Use specific session (overrides current session)
  --no-session                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Force pure random generation (ignore current session) [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Show help and usage information
